inline_comments.message: I am still not sure if this should be the LAST merged commit (ie. from step 4) OR the commit with the "stage-release" ie., from step 3....
The commit ref points to the commit that we want to tag in the git repository. I think it should be the last commit right before the release process started, which is the stage-release commit.
I agree. Also both the log_dir and container versions are also derived from the stage-release commit.
as discussed lets try. I might actually not make any difference. It seem to have worked in DMI-PLugin using the LAST sha instead of the Sha for the stage-commit....

https://github.com/lfit/releng-global-jjb/blob/master/docs/jjb/lf-release-jobs.rst 

Details on container-release parameters:

Required Parameters:	

distribution_type:	Must be "container".
container_release_tag:	The string to use as a Docker tag on all released containers.
container_pull_registry:	The Nexus registry that supplies the staged image(s).
container_push_registry:	The Nexus registry that receives the released image(s).
project:	The name of the project.
ref:	The git commit reference (SHA-1 code) to tag with the version string.
containers:	A list of name and version (tag) pairs that specify the Docker images in the container-pull registry to promote to the container-push registry.

"The git commit reference (SHA-1 code) to tag with the version string." what does the actually mean? In Policy they use a script to get the LAST commit, according to Liam it is for 'future reference' only to indicate when the image was created. whether its the last or 2nd last commit really doesn't mater that much. we waster too much time on it already. I hope for next release this is A) automated or B) we stop fussing about it and accept wither ref...
